perturbation.original_text: Chase will pay Affiliate a fee for each approved credit card account that originates from a link in Affiliate's Website.
perturbation.changed_text: Chase will remit a payment to the Partner for each validated credit card application originating from a hyperlink on the Partner's digital platform.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Affiliate' is replaced with 'Partner,' 'fee' is changed to 'payment,' 'approved credit card account' becomes 'validated credit card application,' 'link' is changed to 'hyperlink,' and 'Affiliate's Website' becomes 'Partner's digital platform'. This creates confusion regarding whether 'Affiliate' and 'Partner', 'fee' and 'payment', and 'approved credit card account' and 'validated credit card application' are referring to the same entities and criteria, potentially leading to disputes over payment terms. This is contradictory in section 5 where it talks about Affiliate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement will commence on the date that the Affiliate Registration Form is approved by Chase and will end when terminated by either party. Either Affiliate or Chase may terminate this Agreement at any time, with or without cause, by giving the other party written or e-mail notice of termination. At the time of termination, any links to Chase's Website must be removed immediately. Affiliate will continue to receive Commission payments for all Approved Accounts placed during the term of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Chase may terminate this Agreement if Affiliate does not comply with the terms and conditions herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Affiliate,' 'fee,' 'approved credit card account,' and 'link.' The changed text replaces these with 'Partner,' 'payment,' 'validated credit card application,' and 'hyperlink,' respectively. The contradicted text repeatedly uses the term 'Affiliate.' If 'Affiliate' and 'Partner' are not the same entity, then the contradicted text regarding the 'Affiliate' is no longer applicable, creating a direct contradiction. The change introduces ambiguity about whether 'Affiliate' and 'Partner' are the same, which contradicts the earlier sections that rely on the term 'Affiliate' without defining it as interchangeable with 'Partner'. The terms 'fee' and 'payment' could also have different meanings impacting payment obligations to the affiliate. It is a misaligned terminology which leads to in-text contradictions.

perturbation.original_text: Affiliate agrees to place Chase's links provided by Linkshare NetworkTM which manages the Affiliate Program ("Links") on its Affiliate's website.
perturbation.changed_text: The Associate shall embed Chase's digital connectors, supplied by Linkshare NetworkTM, the Affiliate Program administrator ("Connectors"), on its Associate's web presence.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Affiliate' is replaced with 'Associate,' and 'Links' with 'Connectors,' 'Affiliate Program manager' is replaced by 'Affiliate Program administrator', and 'Affiliate's website' becomes 'Associate's web presence'. It's now ambiguous if an "Affiliate" is the same as an "Associate," and if "Links" and "Connectors" are identical. This creates a contradiction, especially in Section 8, where the method of tracking is mentioned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Chase will be solely responsible for tracking Approved Accounts made to customers who follow Affiliate's Links. Affiliate will be solely responsible for ensuring that the Links are formatted properly and maintained in a manner, which allows Chase to track such Approved Accounts. No Commission shall be paid if the Approved Account cannot be tracked by Chase's system or if the customer accesses the Chase site through means other than the Links. Chase will provide Affiliate with statements of Approved Account activity at the time Commissions are paid.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Affiliate' to 'Associate' and 'Links' to 'Connectors' creates ambiguity as to whether the terms are interchangeable, directly conflicting with the contradicted text. The contradicted text specifies that Chase is responsible for tracking accounts made to customers who follow 'Affiliate's Links,' and the Affiliate is responsible for ensuring the 'Links' are formatted and maintained correctly. If 'Affiliate' and 'Associate' are not the same, or 'Links' and 'Connectors' are different, then the tracking responsibility becomes unclear. If a customer accesses the Chase site through Connectors and not Links, it's unclear if the affiliate is still entitled to commission, as the original contract explicitly states that 'No Commission shall be paid if the Approved Account cannot be tracked by Chase's system or if the customer accesses the Chase site through means other than the Links.' This ambiguity regarding tracking directly contradicts the terms of payment in the contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, where multiple terms are used without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of determining Affiliate's Commission, an "Approved Account" means any Chase credit card application that is: (i) submitted by any user who clicks on an e-mail, banner or any other advertising material from Affiliate's Website; (ii) is approved by Chase; and (iii) is reported as approved by Chase to Affiliate.
perturbation.changed_text: To ascertain the Vendor's Remuneration, a "Qualified Submission" is defined as a Chase credit card request: (i) tendered by a visitor clicking an electronic message, graphic, or other promotional content from the Vendor's online portal; (ii) sanctioned by Chase; and (iii) communicated as sanctioned by Chase to Vendor.
perturbation.explanation: Here, “Affiliate's Commission” is changed to “Vendor's Remuneration,” and “Approved Account” is changed to “Qualified Submission.” Additionally, “user” is changed to “visitor,” “e-mail, banner, or any other advertising material” is changed to “electronic message, graphic, or other promotional content,” and “Affiliate's Website” is changed to “Vendor’s online portal.” It is ambiguous whether a 'Affiliate' is 'Vendor', or if 'Approved Account' is the same as a 'Qualified Submission'. Due to this the section 13 becomes contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This program is intended for commercial use only. Commissions are payable for Approved Accounts to third parties who access the Chase URL's (marketing pages) through the Links located on Affiliate's sponsoring Web site. Affiliates who use this program to apply for credit cards for their own use are NOT in violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces new terminology ('Qualified Submission', 'Vendor's Remuneration', 'Vendor’s online portal') while altering existing terms ('Approved Account', 'Affiliate's Commission', 'Affiliate's Website'). This creates ambiguity regarding whether 'Affiliate' and 'Vendor' refer to the same entity and if 'Approved Account' and 'Qualified Submission' have the same meaning. This directly contradicts the original text, which specifies 'Affiliate's sponsoring Web site' and 'Approved Accounts' in the context of commissions. Section 13 discusses commissions for 'Approved Accounts' to 'Affiliates'. If a 'Qualified Submission' to a 'Vendor' is now the standard instead of 'Approved Account' for 'Affiliates', it becomes uncertain if Section 13 still applies, creating a direct conflict and thus a strong in-text contradiction.
